title: Ignore ENOENT on umount of SHM
body: We're seeing failures on Master (F29) because of ENOENT when unmounting SHM. I don't see exactly how this is happening, but ENOENT doesn't seem particularly hazardous, so let's just ignore it.

ENOENT A pathname was empty or had a nonexistent component.
Basically if path did not exists.

> ```
>    ENOENT A pathname was empty or had a nonexistent component.
> ```
> 
> Basically if path did not exists.

I know this error but I'd love to know why this is happening to be sure we're not patching away the symptom.
It seems to be specific to `podman checkpoint --export` and F29. I took a peek at the code paths, and I don't see any obvious reason for that.

> It seems to be specific to `podman checkpoint --export` and F29. I took a peek at the code paths, and I don't see any obvious reason for that.

This sounds exotic enough to not worry too much. Thanks, Matt!
